decisions.title: Frame strategy
decisions.decision: Aluminum extrusion base + machined plate gantry (no welding)
decisions.reason: No welding access. Extrusion with through-bolts and shear plates approaches welded stiffness at joints.

decisions.title: Extrusion size
decisions.reason: Wider top face pushes HGR20 rail outboard (better moment arm against lateral cutting load); more mass damps vibration. Cost delta over ~4m total is negligible.

decisions.title: Work envelope
decisions.reason: Large enough for panels, small enough to build stiff on budget. Z 150mm gives workholding clearance.

decisions.title: Gantry beam
decisions.reason: Bending stiffness scales with height³. Tall orientation is 8× stiffer than flat.

decisions.title: Gantry upright joint
decisions.decision: Through-bolt M8 + L-shear plate (10mm Al, machined)
decisions.reason: Through-bolt puts joint in shear not bending. Shear plate carries racking load, not fastener.

decisions.title: Shear plate spec
decisions.decision: Min 80mm legs each direction, 3× M6 per leg, countersunk, helicoils in plate edge
decisions.reason: Bare Al threads in plate edge grain will strip. Helicoils required.

decisions.title: Beam-to-upright connection
decisions.decision: Internal end gussets (80×80mm, 10mm Al) at both ends
decisions.reason: Clamping friction alone will creep. Gussets tie the joint in the racking plane.

decisions.title: Z carriage plate
decisions.decision: 12mm 6061-T6 minimum, all 4 rail block bolt holes used
decisions.reason: Thinner plate flexes under lateral cutting load. Moment arm from spindle CL to rail face amplifies Fx.

decisions.title: Z-axis rail
decisions.decision: HGR15 linear rail on front face of beam
decisions.reason: V-slot wheels insufficient for aluminum work. HGR15 appropriate for Z load.

decisions.title: Z rail surface flatness
decisions.decision: Target ≤0.05mm after CNC skim pass; shim to improve if needed
decisions.reason: 0.02mm ideal but not always achievable. 0.05mm workable for wood and light Al. Raw extrusion is 0.1–0.3mm — unacceptable.

decisions.title: Rail skim procedure
decisions.decision: Machine rail pad on metal CNC after assembly, shim if needed
decisions.reason: Support extrusion at many points with minimal clamp force to avoid spring-back error.

decisions.title: XY linear motion
decisions.decision: HGR20 profiled rail — confirmed, no SBR20 fallback
decisions.reason: Build already spec'd for aluminum (spindle, Mesa card, ballscrews). SBR20 compliance under lateral cutting load would undermine the rest of the spec.

decisions.title: XY drive
decisions.reason: Leadscrews have backlash and efficiency loss unacceptable for Al.

decisions.title: Dual Y axis
decisions.decision: Dual 1605 ballscrews on Y (one per side)
decisions.reason: Single screw on long Y causes racking. Dual screws with gantry squaring required.

decisions.title: Motors
decisions.decision: Closed-loop steppers ~2Nm (Leadshine or JMC) with integrated driver
decisions.reason: Most servo benefit without servo cost. 4 axes: X, Y-L, Y-R, Z.

decisions.title: Spindle
decisions.reason: Water-cooled: quieter, thermally stable for long Al jobs. ER20 allows up to 13mm shank tooling.

decisions.title: Controller
decisions.decision: LinuxCNC + Mesa 7i96S FPGA card over Ethernet
decisions.reason: Deterministic real-time motion. Grbl/Arduino insufficient for this machine size and complexity.

bom.name: 40×120 aluminum extrusion (base rails)
bom.notes: 120mm face up. Qty / total length TBD on final base geometry.

bom.name: HGR15 linear rail 300mm (Z axis)
bom.notes: Mounts on front face of gantry beam

bom.name: HGR20 linear rail 800mm (X axis)
bom.notes: Two rails along gantry beam face; 600mm travel + carriage overrun

bom.name: HGH20CA carriage block (X axis)
bom.notes: 2 per rail — wider spacing resists Z carriage moment

bom.name: HGR20 linear rail 1100mm (Y axis)
bom.notes: One per side of base; 900mm travel + carriage overrun

bom.name: HGH20CA carriage block (Y axis)
bom.notes: 2 per side — pairs per upright foot for angular stiffness

bom.name: 1605 ballscrew 1200mm (Y axis, dual)
bom.notes: One per side — gantry squaring. 900mm travel needs ~1200mm screw to clear BK/BF12 journals.

bom.name: Closed-loop stepper ~2Nm (Leadshine / JMC)
bom.notes: Integrated driver. Axes: X, Y-L, Y-R, Z

bom.name: 2.2kW water-cooled spindle + VFD kit
bom.notes: ER20 collet nose; kit usually includes VFD

bom.name: Mini-ITX PC for LinuxCNC
bom.notes: Needs real-time kernel — verify latency first

bom.name: 10mm 6061-T6 aluminum plate (shear plates)
bom.notes: Min 80mm legs each direction; machined on metal CNC

bom.name: M6 helicoil insert kit
bom.notes: Required — bare Al threads in plate edge grain will strip

bom.name: M8 through-bolts + nuts (gantry uprights)
bom.notes: Through-bolt puts joint in shear, not bending
